context_note: Pretreatment floral anthocyanins and condensed tannins were associated with reduced floral antagonisms; abstract aggregates florivory, larceny, and spiders.
Colour morphs differed in visitation, while display-size response includes more capitula probed but a greater unvisited proportion; no monotone high-vs-low visual ordering.
Red flowers had more petal damage than purple but not a uniform ordered difference across all colour morphs or damage types.
Larger inflorescences attracted more pollinators and pollinators visited more heads.
Female calyces had more phenolics/tannins; moths preferred male buds and larvae did not survive on female buds.
Trait associations differ among pollinator functional groups and populations; abstract does not define one monotone visual effect.
Relative visitation increased with plant height and showed inflorescence-size interactions across hoverfly subfamilies; colour response is subfamily-specific.
Sweet versus skunky scent associations with outcross-pollen quantity and purity reverse or strengthen with pollinator assemblage context.
Nectar alkaloid is explicitly described as a defensive compound; captive bumblebee behavior tended toward reduced visitation, with magnitude dependent on alternative-flower context.
Transgenic lines altered release of floral volatile components; some components prevented florivory and reduced damage. Abstract has no pollinator outcome.

taxon: Cinnyris talatala; Pycnonotus tricolor; Zosterops virens
context_note: Nicotine in artificial nectar is described as an anti-herbivore defensive secondary metabolite; increased nicotine reduced feeding frequency and duration in sunbirds, with species and sucrose-context variation.
Field and laboratory nectar-alkaloid additions reduced bumblebee visits and time per flower only at concentrations far above natural nectar; natural-range treatments had no demonstrated cost.

Pollen and nectar contain steroidal alkaloid zygacine; biologically relevant syrup doses induced prolonged irritation, paralysis, and subsequent deterrence of naive generalist Osmia lignaria foraging. The primary sign reflects the controlled foraging assay, not the five-state visitor survey.
